description:
Your role: You are a log-analyst who has just been given three days of Docker
    container logs that already reside on the filesystem.  
    Everything you need is already under the non-root account “user”.

    Starting point
    1. Directory that holds the raw logs:  
       /home/user/container_logs/
    2. Raw log files inside that directory (nothing else is in that directory):  
       • app_2024-06-01.log  
       • app_2024-06-02.log  
       • app_2024-06-03.log  

    Log-line format (identical in all three files)  
       YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ssZ␣CONTAINER_ID␣LEVEL␣MESSAGE  
       – CONTAINER_ID is always a 12-character hexadecimal string.  
       – LEVEL can be INFO, WARN or ERROR.

    Investigation goals (≈ 5-10 commands required)
    1. Create a new directory /home/user/analysis/ if it does not yet exist.  
       All files listed below must end up inside that directory.
    2. Produce a file called combined.log that contains every line from the three
       source log files, sorted strictly by the full timestamp (oldest first).
       The file must contain exactly one line per original log line, no blank
       lines, no duplicate lines.
    3. Produce a file called error.log that contains only the lines from
       combined.log whose level is exactly “ERROR”.  Keep the
       original order used in combined.log.
    4. Produce a CSV summary file called error_summary.csv that counts how many
       ERROR lines each container_id generated.  
       • The file MUST have a one-line header: “container_id,count”  
       • One data row per unique container_id.  
       • Sort rows by descending count; when two containers have the same count,
         sort those containers’ rows alphabetically by container_id.
    5. Produce a file called top_errors.txt that lists the three most frequent
       ERROR message texts across all days.  
       • Each line must be formatted exactly as:  
         message_text|occurrences  
       • Sort by descending occurrences; ties resolved by ASCII
         alphabetical order of message_text.  
       • Show exactly three lines (even if counts are tied further down).  
       • Only the message text, not the container_id, should be counted.
    6. Finally, create a short Markdown report called README.md that lives in
       /home/user/analysis/.  It must contain, at minimum:  
       • A level-1 heading “# Container Error Analysis”  
       • A bullet list summarising the total ERROR count for each container_id
         in the same order used in error_summary.csv.  
       • A sentence that names the most frequent error message and how many
         times it occurred (you already computed this for top_errors.txt).

    Verification rules
    • Path names and file names must match exactly what is written above.  
    • error_summary.csv and top_errors.txt are strictly checked byte-for-byte,
      including new-line characters (use Unix LF).  
    • combined.log and error.log must each have exactly the expected number of
      lines and the exact text in the correct order.  
    • The test harness will fail if any extra files appear inside
      /home/user/analysis/ or if the expected files are missing or mis-named.

    Hints (not commands)
    • Standard coreutils utilities (cat, sort, grep, awk, cut, uniq, etc.) are
      available.  
    • Work in /home/user; you do not need root privileges.
truth:
<!-- --------------  Ground-truth data for automated grading -------------- -->
    <!-- Contents of the three raw log files that already exist before the task starts -->
    <pre_existing_files>
        <file path="/home/user/container_logs/app_2024-06-01.log"><![CDATA[
2024-06-01T10:15:03Z 4a1b2c3d4e5f INFO Container started
2024-06-01T10:15:05Z 4a1b2c3d4e5f INFO Health check passed
2024-06-01T12:05:47Z 4a1b2c3d4e5f ERROR Connection timeout to database
2024-06-01T12:10:03Z ab12cd34ef56 INFO Container started
2024-06-01T12:15:47Z ab12cd34ef56 ERROR Connection timeout to database
2024-06-01T13:20:10Z 7890abcd1234 INFO Container started
2024-06-01T13:25:22Z 7890abcd1234 ERROR Unexpected EOF while reading response
]]></file>

        <file path="/home/user/container_logs/app_2024-06-02.log"><![CDATA[
2024-06-02T09:00:00Z 4a1b2c3d4e5f INFO Daily scheduled task started
2024-06-02T09:05:00Z 4a1b2c3d4e5f ERROR Failed to pull image
2024-06-02T11:45:15Z ab12cd34ef56 WARN Memory usage high
2024-06-02T12:00:03Z 4a1b2c3d4e5f ERROR Connection timeout to database
2024-06-02T14:30:42Z 7890abcd1234 ERROR Connection timeout to database
]]></file>

        <file path="/home/user/container_logs/app_2024-06-03.log"><![CDATA[
2024-06-03T08:10:11Z ab12cd34ef56 INFO Health check passed
2024-06-03T10:22:47Z ab12cd34ef56 ERROR Failed to pull image
2024-06-03T11:00:00Z 4a1b2c3d4e5f ERROR Connection timeout to database
2024-06-03T12:15:17Z 7890abcd1234 ERROR Unexpected EOF while reading response
2024-06-03T15:45:55Z 7890abcd1234 ERROR Connection timeout to database
]]></file>
    </pre_existing_files>

    <!-- ---------------- Expected artefacts after the agent finishes ---------------- -->
    <expected_files>
        <file path="/home/user/analysis/combined.log" lines="17"/>
        <!-- The exact 17 lines, sorted chronologically -->
        <file path="/home/user/analysis/error.log" lines="10"/>
        <!-- The exact 10 ERROR lines in chronological order -->

        <!-- CSV summary -->
        <file path="/home/user/analysis/error_summary.csv"><![CDATA[
container_id,count
4a1b2c3d4e5f,4
7890abcd1234,4
ab12cd34ef56,2
]]></file>

        <!-- Top 3 error messages -->
        <file path="/home/user/analysis/top_errors.txt"><![CDATA[
Connection timeout to database|6
Failed to pull image|2
Unexpected EOF while reading response|2
]]></file>

        <!-- Minimal required phrases for README.md so the grader can grep -->
        <file path="/home/user/analysis/README.md">
            <!-- Must contain: 
                 1) the heading "# Container Error Analysis"
                 2) bullet list with the three container_id counts in order
                 3) a sentence mentioning "Connection timeout to database" and "6"
            -->
        </file>
    </expected_files>
